verdict: 2026-06-25 19:17:34 PDT — The docs-only/no-hardening direction is sound and matches the repo's settled OPS-6 posture. The root live architecture narrative is also a reasonable shape. One scope issue remains: the sketch updates leaf-facing docs but leaves deployed live protocol/skill surfaces with the same guard-enforcement overclaim, so the documentation truthfulness goal is incomplete.

findings.title: Update the live protocol and skill guard claims too
findings.claim: The sketch corrects the README, hook header, and new narrative, but excludes the deployed live doctrine and skill instructions. `.claude/workflow-protocol.md` is explicitly the deploy source every app reads and still says no direct base-branch commits/pushes are enforced by the guard; `frame` and `close` repeat the same enforcement parenthetical. After `install.sh`, those are the authoritative workflow contracts, so the system would still ship false guard claims even after the README is fixed.
findings.alternative: Keep this doc-only and do not harden the hook, but expand the scope/test notes to update every live, non-historical guard-enforcement statement: `.claude/workflow-protocol.md` rule 5 and the `frame`/`close` hard-constraint parentheticals. Leave historical CHANGELOG/backlog entries alone, and phrase the skill rule as a rule Claude must follow, with the hook described only as a cooperative main/master tripwire.
findings.win: Eliminates the remaining false authoritative statements without touching executable behavior, prevents the new `ARCHITECTURE.md` from contradicting the installed protocol, and keeps the repo's deployed source-of-truth docs aligned with the hook's actual invariant.
